dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7488 IH: CFTC Office of the Chief Economist Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Bresnahan (for himself and Mrs. McClain Delaney) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To create an Office of the Chief Economist at the Commodity Futures Trading Commission.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the CFTC Office of the Chief Economist Act of 2026.

subsection.text: Section 2(a) of the Commodity Exchange Act (7 U.S.C. 2(a)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: There is established in the Commission the Office of the Chief Economist.
The Office of the Chief Economist shall be headed by the Chief Economist.
The Chief Economist shall serve as economic advisor to the Commission and perform functions such as economic analysis, regulatory cost-benefit analysis, and research.

clause.text: The Commission may appoint an individual to a position described in clause (ii)—

subclause.text: in accordance with the statutes, rules, and regulations governing appointments to positions in the excepted service (as defined in section 2103 of title 5, United States Code); and
without regard to any statute, rule, or regulation governing appointments to positions in the competitive service (as defined in section 2102 of such title).

clause.text: A position referred to in clause (i) is a position at the Commission that—

subclause.text: serves the Office of the Chief Economist;
is in the competitive service (as defined in section 2102 of such title); and

item.text: is an economist, research analyst, or data specialist; or

requires specialized knowledge of futures, swaps, or commodities markets, financial and capital market formation or regulation, financial market structures or surveillance, data collection or analysis, or information technology.

clause.text: The appointment of a candidate to a position under authority of this subsection shall not be considered to cause such position to be converted from the competitive service to the excepted service.

subsection.text: Section 15(a) of such Act (7 U.S.C. 19(a)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in paragraph (1), by inserting , after coordinating with the Office of the Chief Economist, before shall;
in paragraph (2)(B), by striking futures markets and inserting markets under the jurisdiction of the Commission;
in paragraph (2), by redesignating subparagraphs (D) and (E) as subparagraphs (E) and (F); and
in paragraph (2), by inserting after subparagraph (C) the following:
